posts.title: 📬 PV Weekly Admin Update 4/17/2026
posts.summary: WEEKLY UPDATE PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL April 17, 2026 Hello Panthers Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happenings and updates from...
posts.author: Araceli Mandujano-Padilla
posts.body:
WEEKLY UPDATE

PIONEER VALLEY HIGH SCHOOL

April 17, 2026

Hello Panthers

Welcome to this week's edition of the Pioneer Valley Newsletter! We're excited to share the latest happenings and updates from our school community to keep you informed and engaged.

posts.title: 🎓 Senior Information - 4/16/26
posts.summary: Good morning! Josten's will be on campus DURING LUNCH ONLY on April 28, to take the last of the cap and gown orders.
posts.author: Lisa Walters
posts.body:
Good morning!

Josten's will be on campus DURING LUNCH ONLY on April 28, to take the last of the cap and gown orders.  They are $65.  You can also go to jostens.com to put in your order.  If you ordered your cap and gown, but weren't here to pick it up on April 2, please go to the administration office to pick it up.  Also - don't forget - all seniors must have a walking partner.  One will wear black and the other will wear teal.
If you need to exchange your cap and gown, please bring it on April 28.  Josten's will be set up outside the administration building.

Prom tickets are on sale until April 30.  The cost is $65, and students need a Prom contract.  If they are bringing a guest, they must have a copy of their guest's driver's license or ID, and an approved guest pass from an administrator at their school.  Please note that all Prom dates must be no older than 20 years old - per SMJUHSD board policy.  Tickets are available at the Business Office from 8:00 - 4:00.

Grad Nite will be on June 3.  Tickets were pre-sold and there are no more available.  Seniors will meet in the student parking lot no later than 5:15.am  Buses will leave at 5:30 am - please make sure that seniors who purchased a ticket are on the bus, as there are no refunds for the trip.  Seniors can take food as long as it is prepackaged and not opened before getting on the bus.  They can bring an EMPTY water bottle.  We will have cases of water on the bus.  Chaperones will be checking bags before seniors board the buses.  We will be back on June 4, around 8 am.  Some advice for seniors: 1) wear close toed shoes - it gets yucky, 2) wear pants, not shorts - it gets really cold down there at 1 in the morning, and on that note, 3) take a jacket, 4) bring a pillow and blankie for the bus, because you will want to be comfortable when you sleep on the ride home - and you WILL sleep on the ride home! 😉 5) You can leave items on the buses, because the buses will be parked and locked until we board them to leave, and you will ride the same bus home.  Ladies, I would advise that if you are taking makeup that you take it in a separate bag and leave it on the bus.  The same with perfume.  Disneyland will trash a $40 lip gloss - seriously, seen it!
Also, just a little information that I know that you won't need, but I do have to disclose.  If you are stopped by the Disneyland police, and taken to the Disney jail, we will miss you, but we will call your parents immediately and you will stay with the nice policemen until they come to pick you up. That is non-negotiable - if you make a bad decision, then you will face the consequences.

Graduation Practice will begin at 1 pm on June 10 IN THE GYM.  All seniors please report there after the last final ends.  THIS IS AN IMPORTANT PRACTICE!!  Please plan to attend, because you will get important instructions about graduation procedure and logistics - about parking for your parents, support for handicapped attendees, rules about items that can be brought, and a timeline for graduation day.  You will come into the gym, SIT WITH YOUR WALKING PARTNER, and then we will begin the practice.  It should be over at 3 pm.

If you have other questions, please contact Lisa Walters at lwalters@smjuhsd.org.

posts.title: 🎓 CAASPP state testing for all juniors begins tomorrow
posts.summary: Dear Students, Staff, and Families, This is a reminder that CAASPP state testing for all juniors begins tomorrow. Please help set students up for success by making sure they: Get a good night’s sle...
posts.author: Merrie Okie
posts.body:
Dear Students, Staff, and Families,

This is a reminder that CAASPP state testing for all juniors begins tomorrow.

Please help set students up for success by making sure they:

 

  • Get a good night’s sleep
  • Arrive on time
  • Bring their fully charged school-issued tablet

We encourage all students to take the CAASPP seriously and do their best. While these assessments represent only one measure of student learning, they play an important role in helping us understand how well students are developing essential skills in reading, writing, and mathematics.
 The CAASPP also provides valuable information for schools and the state. Results help us evaluate the effectiveness of our instructional programs and identify areas of strength as well as opportunities for growth. In addition, this data offers insight into how well we are preparing students with the critical thinking, problem‑solving, and communication skills they need to be successful in college, careers, and an increasingly global society.

 

We appreciate your support in helping our students approach this opportunity with focus and effort.

 

If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

 

Sincerely,

Merrie  Goldin
Assistant Principal of Special Programs
Pioneer Valley High School
805-922-1305 ext. 5707
mgoldin@smjuhsd.org

posts.title: 🎓 Get Ahead This Summer! Allan Hancock Enrollment Workshop 🎓
posts.summary: 🚀 Summer College Credits: Allan Hancock Enrollment Workshop! Help your student get ahead this summer!
posts.author: Israel Hernandez
posts.body:
🚀 Summer College Credits: Allan Hancock Enrollment Workshop!

Help your student get ahead this summer! We are hosting a dedicated workshop to walk students through the Allan Hancock College summer registration process. Whether they want to earn college credit early or explore a new subject, this is the perfect time to start.

The Details

  • 📅 When: Monday, April 13 
  • ⏰ Time: During Lunch
  • 📍 Where: School Library
  • 💻 Requirement: Students must bring their charged school laptop.


Don't let your student miss out, see you in the library this Monday at lunch!

posts.title: 🎓 Please join PVHS & TKJHS for a Motivational Keynote Presentation from Erica Alfaro 4/13/26 @ 6PM in the PVHS Library
posts.summary: Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will be presenting at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library. PVHS, in partnership with TKJHS, is very excited to offer our School Community a chance to...
posts.author: Paul Robinson
posts.body:
Motivational speaker Erica Alfaro will be presenting at PVHS on Monday 4/13/26 at 6pm in the PVHS Library.
PVHS, in partnership with TKJHS, is very excited to offer our School Community a chance to hear an inspirational message from Erica Alfaro. Erica is an award-winning author, keynote speaker, and educational advocate. In 2019, Erica's graduation pictures went viral and made headlines around the world. She was featured in major media outlets such as CNN, The Huffington Post, and Yahoo News. Erica's Harvesting Dreams book is an empowering, inspirational and motivational story. It is a testimony of a girl who went through many adversities and managed to reinvent herself and become the woman she is today. Erica's book won the 2022 International Latino Book Award in the Most Inspirational Young Adult category. Don’t miss this wonderful opportunity to be inspired!   
LINK Watch the 1 minute intro video below!

posts.title: 🎓 Meet with an Allan Hancock College Counselor at PVHS Tuesday, April 21st
posts.summary: Hey Panthers! If you're planning on attending Allan Hancock College in the fall, and you haven't met with an AHC Counselor yet, scan the QR code below to reserve your appointment time for Tuesday, ...
posts.author: Melani Mederos
posts.body:
Hey Panthers!

If you're planning on attending Allan Hancock College in the fall, and you haven't met with an AHC Counselor yet, scan the QR code below to reserve your appointment time for Tuesday, April 21st.

You must meet with an AHC Counselor to get your "First Semester Plan" so you can register for classes in May!

Questions? Please see your PVHS Counselor, Mr. Hernandez in the library, or Mrs. Mederos in the College & Career Center (room 604).
